name: Fairview Golf Course
about: Fairview Golf Course is a public golf course and simulator venue operating under the Distinct Golf Group, established in the late 1950s and acquired by the group in 1966. Located in Lebanon, PA, at the base of the south Gretna mountains, it is a complete golf experience featuring an outdoor course and an indoor golf center with simulators. The venue offers a traditional golf club atmosphere with modern amenities, including club fittings, apparel, and an on-site bar and snack bar. It is designed to cater to golfers of all skill levels, from beginners to serious players, with a focus on providing a challenging yet accessible golfing environment.
why_golfers_like_it: Full-service pro shop with club fittings and apparel
Indoor golf center with simulator capabilities for year-round play
Variety of on-site facilities including bar and snack bar
Host of leagues and tournaments for competitive and social play
Convenient location near major Pennsylvania cities

food_and_drink.notes: On-site bar and snack bar available

simulator_hardware.notes: Simulator technology details not specified on website

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The pro shop offers club fittings, but it's recommended to bring your own clubs for the best experience.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The venue has an on-site bar and snack bar, but outside food and drink policies are not specified.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes on the simulator?
faq.answer: The time for 18 holes on the simulator is not specified on the website.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue is described as being suitable for players of all ages and skill levels.
